title: pinus wallichiana subsp. manangensis
intro: Pinus wallichiana subsp. manangensis, a subspecies of the Himalayan blue pine, is a type of evergreen coniferous tree native to the Himalayas. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Pinus wallichiana subsp. manangensis include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and improve digestion. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
